source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform that brings better, faster, and cheaper financial services to over half the Canadian population who live paycheck to paycheck. We operate in a huge, but overlooked market in a country with the least amount of financial technology innovation in the developed world. Our first act is to become the cheapest and best provider of short-term credit to the 20 million people in Canada who live paycheck to paycheck.

More than 800,000 Canadians have already signed up with Bree and we believe we are just scratching the surface. We are in an exciting place where we have product market fit, explosive growth, and a clear path to becoming one of the most important FinTechs in Canada.

We are at 8-figures of annualized revenue, growing double-digit monthly, profitable, and have had zero voluntary employee churn. We were part of Y Combinator https://www.ycombinator.com/in 2021 and raised a $2M seed round shortly after.





ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for an Senior Product Designer to help shape the next phase of Bree’s product. This is a senior individual contributor role with significant ownership and impact. You’ll work end-to-end on core product surfaces across mobile and web, partnering closely with Product and Engineering to define problems, explore solutions, and ship meaningful improvements for our users.

This role focuses on thoughtful product judgment, clear decision-making, and delivering work that meaningfully improves outcomes for our users and the business.





WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the end-to-end design for major product initiatives, from problem framing and discovery through execution and launch

 - Partner deeply with different stakeholders including Engineers, CX, Business to turn ambiguous ideas into scoped, high-impact releases

 - Design intuitive, accessible experiences across Bree’s mobile and web products, with a strong focus on clarity, trust, and usability

 - Use research, data, and direct customer feedback to inform decisions and iterate on solutions

 - Create and evolve user flows, wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs that are ready to ship

 - Contribute to and help mature Bree’s design system, ensuring consistency while enabling fast iteration

 - Sweat the details while keeping sight of the bigger picture, balancing speed, quality, and impact

 - Advocate for our users, especially those navigating financial stress, by simplifying complexity and reducing friction

 - Collaborate in design critiques and team rituals, and help raise the overall bar for design quality at Bree


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years of experience designing and shipping consumer digital products in fast-paced environments

 - Strong end-to-end product design experience, spanning UX, interaction design, and visual design

 - Proven ability to work through ambiguity and make good decisions with incomplete information

 - Excellent product thinking, with a clear sense of what matters most to users and the business

 - Experience working closely with engineers and understanding technical constraints and tradeoffs

 - Strong visual and interaction design skills, with an eye for simplicity, hierarchy, and polish

 - Comfort using data, analytics, and experimentation to validate and improve your work

 - Proficiency with modern design tools like Figma

 - A portfolio that demonstrates thoughtful problem-solving, real shipped work, and impact

 - Working knowledge of front-end development concepts (e.g. components, state, APIs), and the ability to reason about implementation while designing


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience designing fintech, lending, or other financial products

 - Familiarity with mobile-first products and React Native environments

 - Experience contributing to or evolving a design system

 - Comfort “vibe coding” or prototyping in code when helpful, to explore ideas, validate interactions, or collaborate more closely with engineers


FULL-TIME EMPLOYEE BENEFITS

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform building faster, simpler, and more affordable financial services for Canadians who often live paycheck to paycheck. We operate in a massive market that’s historically been underserved by traditional financial institutions, and we’re building products that help customers access short-term credit with a transparent, user-first experience.

To date, 800,000+ Canadians have signed up for Bree—and we believe we’re still early. We’re at an exciting intersection of product-market fit, rapid growth, and a clear path to becoming one of the most important fintech companies in Canada.

We’re at 8-figures of annualized revenue, growing quickly, and profitable. We were part of Y Combinator (Summer 2021) and raised a $2M seed round shortly after.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to build and scale high-impact, world-class ML systems. You’re passionate about deploying AI solutions, optimizing performance, and driving measurable results. Your work will power critical decisions and shape the future of our technology.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, develop, and deploy end-to-end machine learning pipelines, ensuring efficiency in training, validation, and inference.

 - Implement MLOps best practices, including CI/CD for ML models, model versioning, monitoring, and retraining strategies.

 - Optimize ML models using feature engineering, hyperparameter tuning, and scalable inference techniques.

 - Work with structured and unstructured data, leveraging Pandas, NumPy, and SQL for efficient data manipulation.

 - Apply machine learning design patterns to build modular, reusable, and production-ready models.

 - Collaborate with data engineers to develop high-performance data pipelines for training and inference.

 - Deploy and manage models on cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure) with containerization and orchestration tools like Docker and Kubernetes.

 - Maintain model performance by implementing continuous monitoring, bias detection, and explainability techniques.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Proficiency in Python and familiarity with ML libraries like Scikit-learn, LightGBM, and PyTorch.

 - Strong understanding of machine learning algorithms, including supervised and unsupervised learning techniques.

 - Experience with MLOps tools such as MLflow, Kubeflow, or SageMaker for tracking experiments and automating workflows.

 - Hands-on experience with data manipulation libraries (Pandas, NumPy) and databases (SQL, NoSQL).

 - Knowledge of cloud-based ML deployment and infrastructure management.

 - Ability to implement real-time and batch inference pipelines efficiently.

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to translate business needs into scalable ML solutions.

 - Eagerness to work in a fast-paced environment and continuously refine ML processes for efficiency and accuracy.




BENEFITS:

💰Top of the market compensation for top performers

⚕️Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits plan

🖥 $1,500 annual learning & home-office stipend

🧘🏼 $1,000 annual wellness stipend

🍔 Monthly Lunch Stipend

🚗 Commuter Benefits

🚼Paid Parental leave

🏝20 annual PTO days + unlimited sick days

🚀 Quarterly Team Gatherings

☕ In Office Amenities

source_jobs.title: Creative Strategist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform that brings better, faster, and cheaper financial services to over half the Canadian population who live paycheck to paycheck. We were part of Y Combinator in 2021 and raised a $2M seed round shortly after. We are growing at a double-digit monthly rate and are profitable. Our culture is defined by high ownership, a bias for action, tenacious problem-solving, and striving to be 1% better every day.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a high-impact Creative Strategist to serve as the core architect of our performance engine. We are at a stage of explosive growth, and we need someone to bridge the gap between high-level creative vision and rigorous data analysis.

You aren't just drafting briefs; you are building the systems, managing the workflow, and directing the strategy that drives our 8-figure revenue. You will oversee a talented team of three Creative Strategists, a Video Editor, a Graphic Designer, and a Creator Partnerships Manager. This is a high-impact role for someone who is equal parts data-obsessed analyst, social media visionary, and master project manager.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Team & Pipeline Management: Manage the weekly production pipeline and deliverables across the creative team. You’ll provide consistent feedback to ensure the team’s output evolves alongside platform trends while remaining brand-aligned and relevant to our target customer.

 - Performance Partnership: Collaborate with our Media Buyer to align creative output with account performance. You will be obsessed with the metrics, using data to investigate creative fatigue and platform-specific trends.

 - Creator & Influencer Strategy: Lead the overarching strategy for our creator and influencer program. You’ll build systems to streamline and scale creator selection, concept development, and even invoice management to ensure our partnerships engine runs smoothly and efficiently.

 - System Architecture: Build and maintain the infrastructure of the creative department—from naming conventions and performance trackers to streamlined communication systems between strategy and production.

 - Creative Strategy & Oversight: Provide final approval on all UGC and static briefs. You’ll apply your expert eye to hooks, headlines, and flow to ensure every piece of content is primed for performance before it hits production.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Data-Obsessed: You are obsessed with data and performance metrics. You can look at a Meta dashboard or Motion report and immediately know why a hook failed, why a specific format is winning, and most importantly; how it changes your creative strategy moving forward.

 - Project Management Chops: You have experience managing multi-disciplinary teams (editors, designers, strategists) and keeping a high-volume production engine running on time.

 - Paid Social Fluency: You are social media savvy. You stay ahead of the curve on Paid Meta trends, emerging TikTok formats, and the specific nuances of what makes a "scroll-stopper."

 - Operational Agility: You act with extreme ownership and are willing to roll up your sleeves to get things done. Whether it’s jumping into CapCut to make a quick edit, recording a voiceover, or filming content yourself, you do whatever it takes to get an ad through the finish line.

 - Proven Experience: At least 2+ years of experience in Creative Strategy or Performance Marketing, specifically working on paid social campaigns that drive measurable ROI. Strong preference for experience in the B2C fintech space.


COMPENSATION & CAREER PATH

We are looking for a long-term partner and are flexible with how we start. This role is currently open on a part-time contract basis with the potential to scale into a core full-time member of the Bree team.


INITIAL CONTRACT TERMS

 - Rate: $50 – $70 per hour (commensurate with experience)

 - Commitment: 10 – 20 hours per week to start


FULL-TIME CONVERSION & BENEFITS

For candidates who transition into a per

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Product / Mobile
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform that brings better, faster, and cheaper financial services to over half the Canadian population who live paycheck to paycheck. We operate in a huge, but overlooked market in a country with the least amount of financial technology innovation in the developed world. Our first act is to become the cheapest and best provider of short-term credit to the 20 million people in Canada who live paycheck to paycheck.

More than 800,000 Canadians have already signed up with Bree and we believe we are just scratching the surface. We are in an exciting place where we have product market fit, explosive growth, and a clear path to becoming one of the most important FinTechs in Canada.

We are at 8-figures of annualized revenue, growing double-digit monthly, profitable, and have had zero voluntary employee churn. We were part of Y Combinator https://www.ycombinator.com/in 2021 and raised a $2M seed round shortly after.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Bree is building a pragmatic, high-ownership engineering team that ships customer value quickly and calmly. We care less about perfect craft for its own sake and more about impact, reliability, and momentum. You’ll own features end-to-end across our Lambda-based backend and React Native apps. You’ll make smart assumptions when product specs are thin, be comfortable working around engineering tradeoffs, and iterate fast to get value in customers’ hands. You’ll pair a builder’s mindset with enough testing and observability to keep us moving without breaking things.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Ship full-stack features end-to-end: product discovery, scoping, implementation, and release.

 - Work across a TypeScript/Node serverless stack (AWS Lambda, API Gateway, SQS) and React Native frontends.

 - Make pragmatic calls in ambiguous situations; unblock yourself and others without waiting on perfect specs.

 - Instrument what you ship (logging, metrics, alerts) and add tests to keep releases stable.

 - Improve existing surfaces: refactor opportunistically, pay down tech debt without slowing delivery.

 - Collaborate tightly with design and product on customer-visible outcomes, not theoretical elegance.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Have real startup experience shipping product in lean environments.

 - Have shipped things personally (side projects, OSS, indie apps) and can show a portfolio/GitHub.

 - Are fluent in TypeScript and comfortable across Node, React/React Native, and REST/GraphQL APIs.

 - Have worked with AWS serverless (Lambda/SQS) or equivalent event-driven systems.

 - Use testing pragmatically (Jest, React Native Testing Library, Detox) and integrate into CI.

 - Demonstrate product judgment: you can refine a rough idea into a scoped, high-leverage release.

 - Communicate clearly, default to ownership, and stay even-keeled when wrestling with engineering tradeoffs.
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with mobile release pipelines, over-the-air updates, and app store processes.

 - Observability chops (e.g., structured logging, tracing, performance profiling).

 - Comfort with analytics/experimentation (event models, simple A/Bs) to validate features.

 - Contributions to OSS or public write-ups about projects you’ve shipped.




FULL-TIME EMPLOYEE BENEFITS INCLUDE:

💰Top of the market compensation for top performers

⚕️Comprehensive health, dental, and vision benefits plan

🖥 $1,500 annual learning & home-office stipend

🧘🏼 $1,000 annual wellness stipend

🍔 Monthly Lunch Stipend

🚗 Commuter Benefits

🚼Paid Parental leave

🏝20 annual PTO days + unlimited sick days

🚀 Quarterly Team Gatherings

☕ In Office Amenities

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform that brings better, faster, and cheaper financial services to over half the Canadian population who live paycheck to paycheck. We were part of Y Combinator in 2021 and raised a $2M seed round shortly after. We are growing at a double-digit monthly rate and are profitable. Our culture is defined by high ownership, a bias for action, tenacious problem-solving, and striving to be 1% better every day.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a tenacious Technical Recruiter to help fuel our next phase of growth. As an early member of the talent team, you will be the architect of our engineering and product teams. You are someone who thrives in a high-velocity environment and is excited to establish world-class recruiting standards from the ground up. You will be a brand ambassador for Bree, ensuring every candidate has an exceptional experience regardless of the outcome.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Full-Cycle Recruiting: Own the entire hiring process for technical roles, from initial kick-off with hiring managers to offer negotiation and closing.

 - Strategic Sourcing: Actively hunt for top-tier, passive talent for difficult-to-fill engineering and product roles using LinkedIn Recruiter and creative outbound strategies.

 - Process Engineering: Build and drive the adoption of standardized, scalable hiring workflows that ensure we remain efficient as we scale. Must be proficient with AI.

 - Candidate Experience: Act as the primary point of contact for candidates, providing diligent follow-through and building genuine long-term relationships.

 - Data & Strategy: Utilize a data-driven approach to provide leadership with insights on market trends, pipeline health, and long-term talent acquisition strategy.

 - Employer Branding: Contribute to Bree’s external employment brand, ensuring we are recognized as a top destination for technical talent in Canada.


WHAT YOU’LL NEED

 - 3+ years of experience in full-cycle technical recruiting, ideally within a fast-paced startup or high-growth environment.

 - The "Sourcing Grit": A proven track record of finding and engaging passive candidates who aren't necessarily looking for a new role.

 - Extreme Ownership: A self-starter mentality with the ability to navigate ambiguity and "establish something from nothing" without constant direction.

 - Technical Fluency: Ability to speak the language of engineers and product managers to effectively screen for both skill and culture fit.

 - Exceptional Communication: Impeccable written and verbal skills; you can pitch the Bree vision to a skeptical candidate or a Board member with equal confidence.

 - Tools & Systems: Strong experience with ATS platforms and sourcing tools like LinkedIn Recruiter.




BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary

 - A high-autonomy environment where your impact on company culture is visible every single day.

 - This is a contract, part-time role to start, with opportunity to advance to a full-time role.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Infrastructure
source_jobs.description:
About Bree

Bree is a consumer finance platform that brings better, faster, and cheaper financial services to over half the Canadian population who live paycheck to paycheck. We operate in a huge, but overlooked market in a country with the least amount of financial technology innovation in the developed world. Our first act is to become the cheapest and best provider of short-term credit to the 20 million people in Canada who live paycheck to paycheck.

More than 800,000 Canadians have already signed up with Bree and we believe we are just scratching the surface. We are in an exciting place where we have product market fit, explosive growth, and a clear path to becoming one of the most important FinTechs in Canada.

We are at 8-figures of annualized revenue, growing double-digit monthly, profitable, and have had zero voluntary employee churn. We were part of Y Combinator https://www.ycombinator.com/in 2021 and raised a $2M seed round shortly after.

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer, Infrastructure to own Bree's entire platform layer - from cloud architecture to deployment pipelines to security posture. You'll set the standards, make the foundational decisions, and build the systems that everything else runs on. This is high ownership, high impact work at a profitable, fast-growing Canadian fintech. We are AI-first and leverage the best available tools to make our lives easier, reduce toil, and help us move faster with better judgment.



What You'll Do

 - Design, implement, and maintain infrastructure that ensures the reliability, scalability, and security of Bree's production systems.

 - Own CI/CD pipelines end-to-end - design, maintain, and improve deployment workflows for all production services across Netlify and AWS.

 - Optimize PostgreSQL databases for performance, scalability, and cost-efficiency, including query optimization, indexing strategies, and capacity planning.

 - Implement and enforce best practices for Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using tools such as Pulumi, Terraform, or AWS CloudFormation to achieve consistent and automated deployments.

 - Own Bree's security posture - secrets management, IAM, audit logging, encryption at rest and in transit, and access controls for sensitive customer financial data.

 - Establish robust monitoring and alerting solutions with Grafana/Datadog to ensure proactive detection and resolution of performance bottlenecks and system issues.

 - Drive improvements in system observability, including metrics, logging, and tracing, to enable data-driven decision-making and incident response.

What You'll Need

 - Demonstrated experience building and owning production infrastructure

 - Expertise in serverless architecture using AWS Lambda or similar frameworks.

 - Hands-on experience with Infrastructure as Code tools like Pulumi, Terraform, or AWS CloudFormation.

 - Experience designing and owning CI/CD pipelines from scratch.

 - Security fundamentals: IAM least-privilege, secrets management, encryption, audit logging, and PII handling.

 - Proficiency in TypeScript or Python for automation, scripting, and infrastructure code.

 - Bonus: experience in fintech, regulated data environments, SOC 2, banking rails, payment processors, or financial data integrations.

You’re Excited About This Role Because…

 - You’ll contribute to cutting-edge infrastructure for data-intensive systems that support Canadian consumers.

 - You’re passionate about leveraging advanced technologies to build scalable and efficient platforms.

 - You’ll play a key role in optimizing and maintaining systems critical to the company’s success.

 - You’ll be part of a fast-growing team shaping the future of infrastructure at an innovative company.

 - You’ll have the opportunity to drive impactful changes that enable rapid growth and scalability.

 - You’re excited to collaborate on transformative solutions for reliability, maintainability, and scalability.

Benefits:

💰Top of the m

source_jobs.title: Creative Lead, Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform that brings better, faster, and cheaper financial services to over half the Canadian population who live paycheck to paycheck. We were part of Y Combinator in 2021 and raised a $2M seed round shortly after. We are growing at a double-digit monthly rate and are profitable. Our culture is defined by high ownership, a bias for action, tenacious problem-solving, and striving to be 1% better every day.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a high-impact Creative Lead, Growth to serve as the core architect of our performance engine. We are at a stage of explosive growth, and we need someone to bridge the gap between high-level creative vision and rigorous data analysis.

You aren't just drafting briefs; you are building the systems, managing the workflow, and directing the strategy that drives our 8-figure revenue. You will oversee a talented team of three Creative Strategists, a Video Editor, a Graphic Designer, and a Creator Partnerships Manager. This is a high-impact role for someone who is equal parts data-obsessed analyst, social media visionary, and master project manager.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Team & Pipeline Management: Own the weekly creative pipeline end-to-end. You will decide what gets made, ensure deadlines are hit, and continuously raise the bar by delivering clear, direct feedback that improves output quality across the team.

 - Performance Partnership: Work closely with our Media Buyer to turn performance data into creative direction. You will identify winning patterns, diagnose fatigue, and drive the next wave of concepts based on real signals—not guesses.

 - Creator & Influencer Strategy: Build and scale our creator engine. You will define what “good” looks like, improve creator selection, sharpen briefs, and implement systems that increase output quality while reducing turnaround time and cost.

 - System Architecture: Design and refine the systems that power the creative team. This includes briefing frameworks, naming conventions, tracking systems, and feedback loops that make performance insights actionable and repeatable.

 - Creative Strategy & Oversight: Set the direction for what we test and why. You will review and approve key creative outputs, ensuring every ad has a strong hook, clear angle, and a specific hypothesis behind it.

 - Creative Iteration & Learning Loops: Build tight feedback loops between performance and production. You will run post-mortems, identify patterns in winning and losing ads, and translate those insights into clear next steps for the team.

 


WHO YOU ARE

 - Taste & Judgment: You have strong instincts for what works in paid social. You can quickly identify weak hooks, generic concepts, and missed opportunities—and clearly explain how to improve them.

 - Systems Thinker: You naturally turn chaos into structure. You don’t just produce great work—you build processes that allow others to do the same consistently.

 - Data Fluent: You are comfortable working with performance data (Meta, TikTok, Motion, etc.) and use it to guide decisions. You think in hypotheses, testing, and iteration.

 - Operator Mentality: You take extreme ownership and are willing to get into the work when needed—editing, scripting, recording, or fixing bottlenecks to keep things moving.

 - Non-Traditional Background: You may come from UGC, content creation, or performance marketing—but you’ve built a strong intuition for what converts. You are not a traditional brand marketer.

 - Proven Experience: 2+ years in performance creative, paid social, or content roles where your work directly impacted metrics like CTR, CPA, or conversion rate.
   
    


COMPENSATION & CAREER PATH

We are looking for a long-term partner and are flexible with how we start. This role is currently open on a part-time contract basis with the potential to scale into a core full-time member of the Bree team.


INITIAL CONTRACT TERMS

 - Rate: $50 – $70 per hour (commensurate with exper

source_jobs.title: Lead, Strategy & Operations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

We're building better, faster, cheaper financial services for the half of Canadians who live paycheck to paycheck. YCombinator S21, $2M seed, profitable, growing double digits month-over-month. Team of <20, ~80% engineers, no red tape or politics. We move fast because we have to — and because it's more fun that way.




WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

Our engineering team ships. Our growth engine works. What we don't have yet is the connective tissue: the person who turns "we should do X" into "we did X, here's what we learned, here's what's next."

You'll be the operating system between our vision and our execution. You'll run the OKR cadence, write the briefings that compress a leadership decision from five days to five minutes, kill the meetings that don't earn their seat, automate the reports nobody should be running by hand, and quietly own the projects no one else has time for. Occasionally, you'll PM a feature when the moment calls for it.

This is the role for someone who left consulting or banking because they wanted to build — not just write recommendations and hand them off.




WHAT YOU'LL ACTUALLY DO

 - Run the rhythm. Own OKR planning, weekly business reviews, and the quarterly cadence. Every person on the team should know what they're working on, why it matters, and how it ladders up to the number that matters most. If they don't, that's on you.

 - Be the bridge. Engineering, growth, and operations don't always speak the same language. You translate. You unblock dependencies before they become blockers. You hold the line on priorities when "urgent" tries to crowd out "important."

 - Fix the systems, not the symptoms. When something breaks twice, you redesign it. When a recurring report takes four hours every Monday, you make it run itself. We don't want a process bureaucrat; we want someone who builds machines that don't need them.

 - Be genuinely AI-native. We're an AI-native team and use LLMs and agents heavily — internally, in our tooling, and to replace manual workflows wherever they slow us down. The right person already uses these tools as leverage in their daily work.

 - Write the one-pager. Distill messy, multi-variable problems into something an exec can act on in three minutes. The "so what" matters more than the deck.

 - Take on the work without a natural home. Special projects, vendor negotiations, ad-hoc analysis, the post-mortem nobody wants to write; the in-between projects land with you. You take them from zero to one, then build the system that keeps them running without you.

 - Live in the data. SQL is table stakes. You should be writing your own queries, not waiting on someone else to. You're the kind of person who reaches for the data before the opinion.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3+ years relevant experience. Consulting, investment banking, high-growth startup BizOps/Strategy, or strategic finance.

 - First-principles problem solver. You start with why — why the process exists, what are the true constraints, what's the simplest thing that could work — before jumping to how. You build before you ask permission.

 - Obsessed with data. You don't make claims you can't quantify. You instinctively reach for the query before the opinion, and you're suspicious of any decision that can be measured but isn't.

 - Extreme attention to detail and highly organized. You re-prioritize constantly and context-switch without dropping things. Nothing slips through your system, because you've actually built one.

 - Technically literate. You don't need to ship production code, but you do need to read a SQL query, hold your own with engineers on a tradeoff conversation, and understand technical constraints.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and speed. Startup hyper-growth is messy. You're comfortable with unclear scope, shifting priorities, and decisions made on incomplete information. You don't wait for the org chart to catch up to the work.

 - Very high agency. You don't need to be told twice, and

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer, Underwriting

source_jobs.title: Chief of Staff / BizOps Lead
source_jobs.description:

Be the bridge. Engineering, growth, and operations don't always speak the same language. You translate. You unblock dependencies before they become blockers. You hold the line on priorities when "urgent" crowds out "important."

Fix the systems, not the symptoms. When something breaks twice, you redesign it. When a recurring report takes four hours every Monday, you make it run itself. We don't want a process bureaucrat, we want someone who builds machines that don't need them.

Be genuinely AI-native. We're an AI-native team and lean heavily on LLMs and agents: internally, in tooling, and to replace manual workflows wherever they slow us down. The right person already uses these tools as leverage in their daily work.

Write the one-pager. Distill messy, multi-variable problems into something an exec can act on in three minutes. The "so what" matters more than the deck.

Own the work without a home. Special projects, vendor negotiations, ad-hoc analysis, the post-mortem nobody wants to write — the in-between projects land with you. You take them from zero to one, then build the system that runs without you. Occasionally, you'll PM a feature when the moment calls for it.

Live in the data. SQL is table stakes. You write your own queries. You reach for the data before the opinion.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years relevant experience: consulting, investment banking, high-growth startup BizOps/Strategy, or strategic finance.

 - First-principles problem solver: You start with why - why the process exists, what the true constraints are, what's the simplest thing that could work - before jumping to how. You build before you ask permission.

 - Obsessed with data. You don't make claims you can't quantify. You're suspicious of any decision that can be measured but isn't.

 - Extreme attention to detail and highly organized. You re-prioritize constantly and context-switch without dropping things. Nothing slips through your system, because you've actually built one.

 - Technically literate. You don't ship production code, but you read SQL fluently, hold your own with engineers on a tradeoff conversation, and understand technical constraints.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and speed. Unclear scope, shifting priorities, decisions made on incomplete information - that's the job. You don't wait for the org chart to catch up to the work.

 - Very high agency. You don't need to be told twice, and you don't need permission to fix something obvious. If you spot a problem on Monday, it's a project plan by Tuesday.

 - Genuinely wants to be challenged. You're here to grow and have real impact, not to coast. Growth mindset, takes feedback seriously, allergic to your own complacency.

 - Concise, clear co

source_jobs.title: Content Creator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT BREE

Bree is a consumer finance platform helping Canadians access fast, affordable short-term liquidity without the fees and interest of traditional payday loans.

Hundreds of thousands of Canadians already use Bree, and we’re growing quickly through a strong paid acquisition engine across Meta, TikTok, and other platforms.

We believe great creative drives growth, and we’re looking for someone who’s obsessed with content, storytelling, and internet culture to help us build the next generation of Bree content.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Content Creator (Paid + Organic) to help us produce high-performing short-form video for both paid ads and organic social.

You’ll work closely with our Creative Strategy team to research competitor ads, develop creative concepts, and shoot UGC-style content weekly.

Beyond ads, you’ll also help build Bree’s organic presence on TikTok and Instagram, experimenting with formats, storytelling, and viral playbooks.

This role is ideal for someone who:

 - Lives on TikTok/Reels

 - Understands what makes content go viral

 - Can move quickly from idea → video

 - Enjoys experimenting with creative formats
   

This is a flexible part-time role (10-15 hours/week) with a focus on experimentation, iteration, and learning what works.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Shoot 3 UGC-style ad concepts per week for paid campaigns

 - Film short-form video content for Meta, TikTok, and other performance channels

 - Create, film, and edit 3 organic pieces of content per week for Bree's Instagram and TikTok

 - Research competitor ads, viral formats, and emerging content trends

 - Develop and pitch new creative concepts and storytelling formats

 - Collaborate with the Creative Strategy team to test hooks, formats, and messaging

 - Help build and evolve Bree’s organic social presence

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Obsessed with content and viral internet culture

 - Strong understanding of TikTok / Reels content playbooks

 - Comfortable filming yourself on camera

 - Strong storytelling instincts and ability to craft engaging narratives quickly

 - High agency, you take ownership and move quickly without waiting for direction

 - Strategic thinker who understands how creative drives growth

 - Experience with short-form editing tools (CapCut, TikTok editor, etc.)

 - Organized and reliable with weekly deliverables

 


COMPENSATION

 - Monthly retainer + performance-based bonus

 - Flexible schedule

 - Fully remote, preference for someone in the GTA who can film content in Toronto

 

Pro Tip: Applicants who include links to content they’ve created (TikTok, Instagram, etc.) will be prioritized.

notes: High-growth consumer finance platform with strong engineering presence and product focus.
